matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: An observed IGF1R variant places this sample in the curated IGF1R research context. The bundled IGF1R records are intended as research and pathway-context markers, not as a diagnostic-grade interpretation set. Pathogenic IGF1R coding variants do exist in growth-disorder genetics, but the initial local database added here focuses on commonly studied polymorphisms and regulatory context relevant to the current SNP-and-methylation workflow. Commonly discussed IGF1R SNPs in this local database are best read as pathway modifiers, cohort markers, or regulatory correlates rather than as high-penetrance pathogenic variants.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: IGF1R is heavily studied in cancer biology because altered IGF1 signaling can support proliferation, survival, and therapy resistance.
Human cohort work has also linked common IGF1R polymorphisms to aging-related phenotypes, hypertensive-pregnancy follow-up outcomes, cardiovascular traits, and selected birth or growth phenotypes.
The strongest methylation evidence in the local database is regulatory and biomarker-oriented rather than diagnostic on its own.

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a IGF1R variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. IGF1R methylation is best interpreted as regulatory context that may reflect local promoter accessibility or broader IGF-axis state rather than as a stand-alone disease call. The bundled EPIC subset includes promoter-adjacent CpG-island probes and many 5'UTR or gene-body probes, so this workbench should be read as a locus-level methylation view rather than a one-to-one proxy for every published IGF1R epigenetic assay. The bundled IGF1R records are intended as research and pathway-context markers, not as a diagnostic-grade interpretation set. Pathogenic IGF1R coding variants do exist in growth-disorder genetics, but the initial local database added here focuses on commonly studied polymorphisms and regulatory context relevant to the current SNP-and-methylation workflow. Commonly discussed IGF1R SNPs in this local database are best read as pathway modifiers, cohort markers, or regulatory correlates rather than as high-penetrance pathogenic variants.
